{{Quote|A jinxed vessel if ever there was one!|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Queen of Empire''''' was a [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liner]] that belonged to [[Haj Shipping Lines]].

==Characteristics==
At 2,000 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long, it provided luxury accommodations for 5,000 passengers.<ref name="RD">''[[Rebel Dawn]]''</ref> Equipped especially for the upper echelons of society, the ''Queen of Empire'' had all the amenities available for beings of wealth, including lavish casinos, exercise rooms, day spas, shopping areas, and exotic restaurants. Passengers would travel between decks using [[transfer tube]]s. Its nightclubs featured many famous bands, including the [[Rughja]] orchestra-band called [[Umjing Baab and His Swinging Trio]], which played in the [[Star Winds Lounge]]. The ''Queen of Empire'' also had a [[gaming parlor]], where passengers could go to [[Gambling/Legends|play games of chance]].

==History==
An unfortunate stigma was associated with the ''Queen of Empire'' since it had been raided several times by pirates and even agents of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] during its long history navigating the space lanes. Some referred to the vessel as being jinxed or cursed.

At approximately [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Commander [[Bria Tharen]] was using the luxury liner as transport from her [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] cell on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] to [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] where she hoped to enlist aid from some of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] lords in fighting the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. It was then that [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] captured her and was ready to transport her back to [[Teroenza]] who had offered the bounty on her head. Fortunately for her and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] who had gotten mixed up in the mess when he asked Bria out for a dance, the pirate [[Drea Renthal]] had knocked the liner out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] between Corellia and its layover at [[Gyndine/Legends|Gyndine]] by dragging a massive asteroid through the ''Queen of Empire's'' path. When her pirates raided the vessel, they encountered Boba Fett and his two captives and convinced the bounty hunter to let them go by paying off the 100,000 credit bounty using money they had acquired from raiding the vessel.

The liner was also stopped during the Yuuzhan Vong invasion of the galaxy. During this era, the ''Queen of Empire'' was serving to transport refugees from [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] to the [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]], moving helpless sentients away from the invasion corridor. The ''Queen's'' many luxuries were converted to refugee usage, closing down most of their restaurants and nightclubs for use as living space for the displaced masses. Agents of the [[Peace Brigade]] pulled the ship out of hyperspace just short of Bilbringi using a [[dovin basal]] in an attempt to capture a defecting priestess named [[Elan]]. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was also aboard, and [[Reck Desh]] forced him into a deactivated transfer tube drop shaft, but Solo was able to grab [[Droma]]'s tail and survive.

The ''Queen of Empire'' had a sister ship also produced by Haj Shipping Lines, named ''[[Star of Empire]]''.

==Continuity==
Contrary to how the ''Queen of Empire'' was first mentioned in ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' (being "fully two kilometers long, and equipped to carry five thousand passengers"), ''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' describes her as being a diminutive 150 meters in length, with room for 600 passengers only.<ref name="NJOS">''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]''</ref> The later, however, also contradicts the description of her sister ship ''[[Star of Empire]]''.
